# Expected files after /mcp-docs runs
#
# NONE. /mcp-docs is a read-only documentation-lookup skill — it queries MCP
# documentation providers (Context7) and returns a structured summary to stdout
# (or records the source + decision in working notes / plan / review). It MUST
# NOT create or modify project files.
#
# Presence of ANY file in the output dir after a /mcp-docs run is a contract
# violation. Verify manually:
#
#   cd tests/fixtures/fixture-21-mcp-docs/output/
#   ls -la       # should show only . and .. (plus optional `.rubric-status`)
#
# /mcp-docs MUST NOT:
#   - Write / Edit project files (it's a read-only lookup)
#   - Send secrets / proprietary source / credentials / private customer data
#     to documentation tools
#   - Block tiny local fixes when the relevant API behavior is already proven
#     by tests or code
#
# /mcp-docs MAY:
#   - Resolve a library ID and query MCP docs (Context7) with a narrow question
#   - Fall back to official project docs when MCP is unavailable (stating so)
#   - Print the output shape (DOC SOURCE / VERSION OR LIBRARY ID / DECISION /
#     IMPLEMENTATION IMPACT / FOLLOW-UP RISK)
#
# Validation deferred (status: pending) — same bucket as fixture-15-advisor
# (read-only, external MCP tool + stdout). Manual verification via notes.md.
